myopenmath.statement:
A movie theater company wants to see if there is a difference in the variance of movie ticket sales in New Orleans and Milwaukee per week. They sample 18 sales from New Orleans (group 1) and 18 sales from Milwaukee (group 2) and count the number of tickets sold over a week. Test the claim using a significance level of \displaystyle\alpha =0.10. Assume that movie sales are normally distributed.
New OrleansMilwaukee
542561
561554
542560
537562
544553
535554
560557
545558
537557
548556
556556
558559
573550
566564
525560
570550
563553
543560
What are the correct hypotheses? Note this may view better in full screen mode. Select the correct symbols in the order they appear in the problem.
{H}_{0}:
σ;8321
σ;8321;sup2
s;8321
μd
μ;8321
x̅;8321
p;8321
≠
/label
=
≥
≤
σ;8322
μ;8322
σ;8322;sup2
p;8322
0
x̅;8322
s;8322
{H}_{1}:
σ;8321;sup2
x̅;8321
p;8321
μd
s;8321
σ;8321
μ;8321
/label
≤
≠
=
≥
s;8322
σ;8322;sup2
p;8322
σ;8322
μ;8322
0
x̅;8322
Based on the hypotheses, compute the following:
Round answers to at least 4 decimal places.
The test statistic is =
The left tail critical value is =
The right-tail critical value is =
The decision is to
Fail to reject the null hypothesis
Accept the alternative hypothesis
Reject the null hypothesis
Reject the claim
.
The correct summary would be:
There is not enough evidence to reject the claim
There is enough evidence to reject the claim
There is not enough evidence to support the claim
There is enough evidence to support the claim
that there is a difference in the variance of movie ticket sales in New Orleans and Milwaukee per week.

ul.li: D: There is enough evidence to support the claim
